texts.full_text: ystolic pulmonary artery pressure (sPAP), and disease-specific biomarkers. One hundred twenty patients (60.9 ± 8.8 years; 75.0% men; LVESV: median [interquartile range] =53 mL) were enrolled with a follow-up of >90%. There was no difference in change from baseline to crossover between groups for the primary end point (-19 ml, -11/-32 vs-17ml,-12/33 P=0.81). Changes favoring BiV-CRT included LVEF (7% [2%-13] versus 1 [-5%/6], p = .02), RV dimensions and function at baseline but not crossover period (+10 mm[4 to14]/+3, -8/-18 vs +9mm [+0.08/15/+11ml; P=ns). LVESV decreased in both groups (BiVR-CRT: 25 ml [-39%]; LVC-RCT:-17 (-33%) at baseline and-23(-43)and –14(–32), respectively, p = ns]. Both pacing strategies resultedin a decrease of MR by ≥1 grade during follow-up. BiV vs LV CRT had similar effects on changes in disease-specific biomarkers (natriuretic peptide A: 11 pg/mL [-15/38] versus -3 (-9)/26; P=ns) and systolic pulmonary artery pressure (+6 [0-12]/+4 mm Hg, p = ns). Both pacing strategies had similar effects on structural cardiac remodelling in patients with moderate to severe heart failure. A significant reduction of MR was seen irrespective the numberof leads implanted. Impacto de la estimulación biventricular frente a monoventrículo en pacientes con insuficiencia cardiaca: análisis del ensayo Evaluation Resynchronization Therapy for Heart Failure (EARTH).

texts.full_text: I love about this blog, is that a lot of my readers, like me, work odd hours. I enjoy seeing the timestamps on the emails and comments; a surprising number of them originate between 0100 and 0400 hours. I guess a lot of ER types get bored and read blogs on the night shift.One of the things I don't like about my job is that I have to work nights. Don't get me wrong -- I worked nights exclusively for a few years before I had kids, by choice. I like the pace of night shifts, I like the occasional down time, I like the camaraderie that the night staffs always seem to enjoy, and I even learned to like the traditional post-night shift team meal of pancakes and beer. That was when I was working all nights. Now I just work the same fraction of nights as the other docs in our grou...p (our group is pretty split on night shifts, but most people only take them every 2-3 months). My kids keep me up at weird hours too; they're always wanting to know some silly thing or needing a snack and waking their poor old dad. texts.full_text: an observational study in India. The clinical presentation of cardiac abnormalities in HIV-infected patients may be atypical or masked by concurrent illnesses that lead to misdiagnosis or they remain undiagnosed; therefore, this study was aimed to determine the frequence of cardiac abnormalities in HIV-positive patients and to analyze the risk factors associated with the development of these abnormalities. Cardiac abnormalities were found in 28 patients (40%). Cardiac abnormalities are a common cause of morbidity and mortality in the population of HIV-infected patients; therefore, it is important to detect them in time and treat them appropriately to avoid fatal consequences. The study also assessed the relationship between cardiac abnormalities and clinical parameters, including serum magnesium, calcium, potassium, phosphorus, lipid profile and blood sugar. Cardiac abnormalities were found in 28 patients

texts.full_text: (3 January 1942 – 27 April 2014) was a Portuguese lawyer, writer, translator and politician, son of Francisco José da Graça Moura and wife Maria Teresa Amado da Cunha Seixas Navarro de Castro, of Northern Portugal bourgeoisie. He was a Member of the European Parliament for the Social Democratic Party–People's Party coalition; part of the European People's Party–European Democrats group. He married three times, firstly in 1964 to Maria Fernanda de Carvalho de Sá Dantas, secondly in 1985 to Clara Crabbé da RoSilva, and thirdly in 2005 to Maria José de Sousa Fernandes. He had three children from his first marriage and three from his second marriage. He was also a great-grandson of the first President of the Portuguese Republic, Manuel Teixeira Gomes. Vasco Graça Moura was born on January 3, 1942, in Lisbon, Portugal. He studied law at the University of Lisbon and later pursued a career in law, politics, and literature. He was a prominent figure in Portuguese politics, serving as a Member of the European Parliament for the Social Democratic Party–People's Party coalition. He was also a member of the European People's Party–European Democrats group. In addition to his political career, Graça Moura was a prolific writer and translator. He wrote several books on Portuguese history and culture, and his works have been translated into several languages. He was also a regular contributor to Portuguese newspapers and magazines. Graça Moura was married three times. His first marriage was to Maria Fernanda de Carvalho de Sá Dantas in 1964, and they had three children. His second marriage was to Clara Crabbé da Silva in 1985, and they had three children as well. His third marriage was to Maria José de Sousa Fernandes in 2005. texts.full_text: re friends than a Super Lotto winner, only they’re richer in spirit and more authentic. There are few, if any airports that are home to a greater variety of aircraft—from restored and still-operative vintage airplanes, to one-of-a-kind and last-of-a-kind aircraft, to aerobatic planes, to privately owned military fighters and bombers that share the hangars and tarmac with the most modern business jets. It didn’t take long for Terwilliger’s passionate perfection for the subject to rub off on others, including motion picture and television industry personalities Sydney Pollack, Tony Bill, Lorenzo Lamas, Fess Parker, Leon Kaplan, Hal Fishman and Paul Moyer. All of them are pilots who cross paths over Southern California skies—and Terwilliger has interviewed them, using Panavision’s cutting-edge High Definition camera. Those plugged into the aviation world will recognize many whose VNY memories Terwilliger has captured, including Pollack, Bill, Lamas, Moyer, Fishman, astronaut Gordon Cooper, Clay Lacy, AOPA President Phil Boyer, Barry Schiff, Sean Tucker, Matt Jackson, Willis Hawkins, Fred Murphy, John O’Crowley and the “voice” of the VNY control tower, Phil Aune. They’re among the 70 enthusiasts who have stepped up to the camera and told their heartwarming stories about VNY. More than 150 people and businesses around the field have thrown their hats into the project. L to R: Captain Jon Witkin, an Air Force F-16 fighter pilot, and Brian J. Terwilliger grew up together in Connecticut, fascinated with airplanes. “Four years ago, I was inspired to tell this story before the world loses these aviators,” said Terwilliger. “I wanted to archive the 75 years of history, people and aircraft of this fascinating airport that I call home. texts.full_text: eople. Governance of the use of force had improved after some young people had been badly hurt two years previously. The segregation unit was cramped and run-down, and although relationships with staff were generally good, the regime was inadequate, especially for the few young people held there for lengthy periods. Like all juvenile prisons, Hindley held some very unhappy young people. There had been a very sad self-inflicted death at the beginning of 2012, and the establishment had taken early action as a result of the findings of an investigation into the incident. The number of self-harm incidents remained high (although relatively low level) and, despite the reduction in the population, the number of incidents each month had grown by 18% over the previous year. However, we were not assured that the drive to learn and implement lessons from the death in 2012 was being sustained, and some staff were not clear about their responsibilities in this area. We were concerned that the sheer volume of violent and self-harm incidents threatened to be overwhelming. For the most part, individual incidents were dealt with well but there needed to be more complete strategic oversight of the entire picture that made the links between bullying and self-harm and kept responses to both perpetrators and victims under review. The safeguarding committee with its external membership appeared to be best placed to do this. texts.full_text: rate of 308 nm laser ablation in ocular tissue and elucidate the photoablation mechanisms involved, 85 full-thickness ab interno sclerostomies were created in six human donor eyes using an 800-microns-diameter quartz optical fiber. A laser pulse duration of 135 ns, fluence of 31 mJ/mm2, and a fixed repetition rate between 5 and 40 Hz were used for 38 sclerostomies; the remaining 47 sclerostomies were completed at various laser settings during initial experimentation. Surprisingly, the numbers of pulses required for complete penetration of the optical fiber through the fixed tissue thickness were not constant as expected but decreased nonlinearly with increasing repetition rate. This demonstrRate-dependent, linear photoablation of ocular tissue at 308 nm: To further understand this phenomena and identify a correlation between pulse repetition rates and the effectiveness of beam ablation in human eyes, we conducted an extensive research involving both experimental methods and experimental data. The findings obtained from our investigation showed that lowering the wavelength (Hz) contributed to reduced power expenditure per unit area while providing consistent penetration outcomes for varying tissue characteristics within each eye studied – showing a complex relationship between thermal effects caused by repetitive laser exposure at various intervals as well as mechanical factors inherent in ocular cells themselves which can affect ablation dynamics. In order to explore these interactions more thoroughly, we employed several experimental methods such as high-resolution imaging of the ablated areas post treatment and monitoring changes both spatially from each eye globe's surface area under investigation while additionally monitoring spatial patterns over successive rounds/sequences involving different signals delivered at different intervals varying from 5 MHz up through higher rates like those utilized in clinical settings today (40Hz). texts.full_text: via activating the Bcl-2/Bax-mediated signaling pathway. Neohesperidin, a flavonoid compound found in high amounts in Poncirus trifoliata, has free radical scavenging activity. For the first time, our study indicated that neohesperidin also induces cell apoptosis in human breast adenocarcinoma MDA-MB-231 cells, which was possibly mediated by regulating the P53/Bcl-2/Bax pathway. MDA-MB-231 cells were subjected to treatment with neohesperidin. MTT and Trypan blue exclusAssay results showed that cell viability decreased in a dose dependent manner after 48 hours of exposure, while the percentage apoptosis increased significantly compared against control groups at each concentration tested as determined through flow cytometry analysis using Annexin V-FITC/PI staining method. You are here: Home / Interviews / “It’s always an exploration”: Talking Burma with 2014 Rhodes Scholar Courtney Wittekind “It’s always an exploration”: Talking Burma with 2014 Rhodes ScholarCourtney Wittekind. Photo courtesy of the University of Mississippi. In late November, Oxford-bound Courtney Wittekind sat down in her parents' living room to talk about winning a prestigious scholarship and what she hopes for Myanmar's future as it undergoes political changes unprecedented since 1962—the year military rule began there with General Ne Win’s coup d‘état. Wittekind, who graduated summa cum laude from the University of Mississippi in May after double majoring in international studies (with a focus on Southeast Asia) and religious studies/history while minoring in anthropology-sociology as well Chinese language & culture—will pursue her doctorate at Oxford’s St Cross College. She will study under Dr David Scott, whose research focuses include Burmese Buddhism since 1824; the role of religion within ethnic nationalism (especially among Karen Buddhists); and religious responses to modernity across Southeast Asia more broadly. Wittekind's interest in Burma began with a high school project on human rights abuses against Rohingya Muslims living there—an assignment that led her, as she puts it “down this rabbit hole” into the country’s history of ethnic conflict (and what many call genocide) and its current political situation under Aung San Suu Kyi. She visited Myanmar for first time in 2013 to work with a non-governmental organization called Education Support Network that provides educational opportunities—including scholarships, teacher training programs as well English classes through local

texts.full_text: hearing loss-related quality of life (HL-QOL) in comparison with SPs who did not participate in a group class. Additionally, to determine whether PHLs of SPs who participated in a group audiological rehabilitation (AR) class had significantly improved mood, reduced stress, improved marital communication, and better HL-QOL scores in comparison with PHLs whose SPs did not participate in a group class. A randomized controlled study. A total of 72 individuals participated in the study, 36 PHLs and 36 SPs. The PHLs were hearing aid users or cochlear implant users; the SPs had normal or near normal hearing. PHLs in the control group participated in a traditional group AR program designed for patients with sensorineural deafness, emphasizing individual counseling focused on managing their own symptoms and coping strategies specific to living with hearing loss. In contrast, both groups of spouses received different interventions as part of this study. The experimental arm consisted entirely of training aimed at communication techniques tailored specifically toward enhancing relationships between the PHLs and SPs along with psychosocial exercises targeting emotional well-being within that context. All participants completed several standardized questionnaires prior to intervention initiation (baseline), immediately following completion, six months later as a short-term follow-up assessment point 1 month post-intervention cessation. Longitudinal measurement points were chosen based on previous research indicating significant improvements occur during this timeframe for similar programs addressing dyadic adjustment after hearing loss interventions [references can be included here]. The questiOnnaire battery includes instruments specifically crafted to gauge moods (e.g., Beck Depression Inventory, State-Trait Anxiety Index - STAI), perceived stress levels via the Perceived Stress Scale-14 or equivalent tool validated within a diverse population. Marital communication quality was assessed utilizing questionnaires such as dyadic adjustment scale which assesses both satisfaction with marriage and interpersonal problem-solving efficacy (e.g., Resolution of Interpersonal Conflicts in Marriage Inventory -RICMIN), Lastly, HL-QOL measures were chosen focusing on how their daily lives are impacted by hearing impairment from an outsiders' perspective – specifically the Impact Questionnaire for Hearing Impairment Version II was utilized.
